title: 6.2 Cycling of Materials in an Ecosystem
description: Biogeochemical cycles overview — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: What does an ecosystem refer to?
options: Only living organisms in an area
The complex of living organisms, their physical and chemical environment, and all their interactions in a particular unit of space
Only non-living components of an environment
questions.correctAnswer: The complex of living organisms, their physical and chemical environment, and all their interactions in a particular unit of space

questions.question: What are the two main components of an ecosystem?
What does the biotic component of an ecosystem consist of?
What does the abiotic component of an ecosystem comprise?
Why is it essential for nutrient materials to be continuously reused in an ecosystem?
options: Because materials are found in unlimited amounts
Because materials are found in limited amounts
Because organisms produce too much waste
Because the sun provides all nutrients
questions.correctAnswer: Because materials are found in limited amounts

questions.question: Between which components does cycling of materials occur?
How do materials move around within an ecosystem?
options: When organisms feed, breathe, respire, and excrete
questions.correctAnswer: When organisms feed, breathe, respire, and excrete

questions.question: What happens to nutrients when waste products or dead bodies are decomposed?
What does animal excreta become after being broken down by decomposers?
options: A vital nutrient like nitrogen and phosphorus for plant growth
questions.correctAnswer: A vital nutrient like nitrogen and phosphorus for plant growth

questions.question: What are the decomposers commonly found in soil?
What key role do decomposers play in the ecosystem?
How does digestion occur in decomposers compared to ingesters?
options: Outside the living body in the soil through extracellular digestion
They do not digest at all
questions.correctAnswer: Outside the living body in the soil through extracellular digestion

questions.question: What do decomposers release onto dead matter to digest complex organic matter?
What is the result of extracellular digestion by decomposers?
options: Complex organic matter is built up
Complex organic matter is broken into simpler and smaller molecules that are then absorbed
questions.correctAnswer: Complex organic matter is broken into simpler and smaller molecules that are then absorbed

questions.question: What do the products released by decomposers accumulate to form in the soil?
How does the nutrient pool from decomposers affect the soil?
options: It increases soil fertility and plant productivity
questions.correctAnswer: It increases soil fertility and plant productivity

questions.question: In addition to water, how many elements (nutrients) cycle through the ecosystem?
Which four elements cycle through the ecosystem besides water?
What path do these cycling elements take?
options: From the non-living environment to living organisms and back to the non-living environment
questions.correctAnswer: From the non-living environment to living organisms and back to the non-living environment

questions.question: What is a bio-geochemical cycle?
options: A cycle involving only geological processes
A path that includes biological, geological, and chemical processes forming cycles
A cycle involving only chemical processes
A cycle involving only biological processes
questions.correctAnswer: A path that includes biological, geological, and chemical processes forming cycles

questions.question: A waste product from one organism can serve as what for another organism?
What can a waste product be a rich source of instead of polluting the environment?
